title: Visual Comparison of Open and Closed Spheres in a Metric Space
description: A side-by-side visual representation showing the defining characteristics of an open sphere (neighborhood) and a closed sphere in a metric space, highlighting the key difference in their boundaries.
elements: An open sphere S_r(x) = { y ∈ X | d(x,y) < r }
A closed sphere S̄_r(x) = { y ∈ X | d(x,y) ≤ r }
A boundary point at distance r
relationships: The open sphere contains all points strictly within the radius r, visualized with a dashed boundary line to indicate these points are not included. The closed sphere contains all points within and including the boundary of radius r, visualized with a solid boundary line to indicate these points are included.
